workload.replicas.note: Supported. Overridable per environment via mergeEnvironment.

workload.terminationGracePeriodSeconds.note: WRONG DEFAULT: Chart defaults to 10s. 98.8% of customer environments use 40s. A 10s grace period causes data loss during rolling updates on busy dotCMS instances.

workload.image.repository.note: Supported. Default is a test image — must be overridden in production values.

workload.image.tag.note: Supported. Required parameter — must be provided per customer.

workload.javaHeap.note: Supported via javaMemory value. Chart appends -Xmx{javaMemory}G to CMS_JAVA_OPTS.

affinity.type.note: CRITICAL GAP: Chart only supports boolean — true=required, false=none. There is NO support for 'preferred' (soft) anti-affinity. 81.2% of customer environments use preferred. The chart either over-enforces (required blocks scheduling when only 1 node available) or under-enforces (false = no anti-affinity at all). Fix: add affinity.type enum: none|preferred|required.

affinity.topologyKey.note: Hardcoded in template. Correct value. No override needed.

lifecycle.preStopSleepSeconds.note: CRITICAL BUG: preStop sleep is hardcoded to 1 second (sleep '1'). 100% of customers that define preStop use 20 seconds. 1 second is insufficient to drain in-flight requests before SIGTERM, causing dropped connections during pod termination. Fix: make configurable via values (e.g. lifecycle.preStopSleepSeconds: 20).

lifecycle.postStart.note: Supported. Rendered when useLicense=true. Correct behavior.

ingress.scheme.note: Hardcoded in ingress template. Matches convention.

ingress.targetType.note: Hardcoded in ingress template. Matches convention.

ingress.sslPolicy.note: Supported but no default set — required field with empty default. Should default to ELBSecurityPolicy-FS-1-2-Res-2020-10 (96.1% adoption).

ingress.class.annotation_and_spec_confli.note: BUG: Template sets BOTH 'kubernetes.io/ingress.class' annotation (deprecated) AND 'spec.ingressClassName' simultaneously. Customers use annotation only (96.9%). Adding spec field is new behavior that may conflict with older ALB controller versions. Fix: use spec.ingressClassName only, remove deprecated annotation.

ingress.healthcheck.path.note: WRONG DEFAULT: Chart routes ALB healthcheck to readinessProbe path (/dotmgt/readyz, port 8090). 82.4% of customers use /api/v1/appconfiguration on port 8082 (the main app port). The management port (8090) and readiness path are internal — using them for ALB healthcheck creates a dependency on the management port being exposed. Fix: add ingress.alb.healthcheck.path with default /api/v1/appconfiguration on port 8082.

ingress.healthcheck.intervalSeconds.note: WRONG DEFAULT: Chart defaults to 10s interval. 83.1% of customers use 30s. 10s is too aggressive and increases ALB cost.

ingress.healthcheck.timeoutSeconds.note: WRONG DEFAULT: Chart defaults to 5s. Customers use 2s. Minor but should align.

ingress.healthcheck.healthyThresholdCoun.note: WRONG DEFAULT: Chart defaults to 3. Customers use 2. Faster healthy detection.

ingress.tga.deregistration_delay.note: WRONG DEFAULT: Chart defaults to 60s. Customers use 30s. Higher deregistration delay increases downtime during deployments.

ingress.tga.stickiness_enabled.note: WRONG DEFAULT: stickySessions.enabled defaults to false. All customers use sticky sessions (dotCMS stateful sessions require it). Note: chart has DUPLICATE stickiness config in both hosts.stickySessions and targetGroup.stickiness. The targetGroup path defaults to true (correct) but hosts.stickySessions defaults to false. The helper at _helpers.tpl:548 uses the hosts.stickySessions path — bug.

ingress.tga.slow_start.note: WRONG DEFAULT: Chart defaults to 120s. Customers use 60s.

ingress.lba.access_logs_enabled.note: WRONG DEFAULT: Chart defaults to false. All customers have access logs enabled. S3 bucket name follows pattern dotcms-{region}-alb-access-logs.

ingress.lba.idle_timeout.note: WRONG DEFAULT: Chart defaults to 3600s. Customers use 600s.

ingress.wafAclArn.note: Supported. Empty default is correct (34.5% adoption — opt-in is right).

ingress.securityGroups.note: Supported. Required parameter for ALB deployments.

ingress.vantaTags.note: BUG: VantaNonProd tag is hardcoded to 'true' for ALL environments including production. Should be conditional: VantaNonProd=false for prod environments.

customerLevel.volumes.accessPointPerEnv.note: EXPRESSIBLE but undocumented: The mergeEnvironment helper deep-merges env-specific values, so 'volumes.shared.accessPoint' CAN be overridden per environment by placing it inside environments.{envName}.volumes.shared.accessPoint. This is not documented and the values.yaml schema only shows a single accessPoint. Must be documented explicitly and validated in Spec 05.

customerLevel.volumes.volumeHandle.note: Supported. Single EFS filesystem handle per customer (all envs share same FS).

customerLevel.serviceAccountName.note: NAMING MISMATCH: Chart generates '{customer}-app-sa' by default. Customers use '{customer}-sa' (without -app- infix). serviceAccountName can be overridden via values but must be set explicitly for all 84 customers. Fix: change default pattern or document required override.

customerLevel.iamRoleArn.note: Supported via service-account.yaml annotations. Required parameter.

customerLevel.networkPolicies.standard.note: Supported. All 4 standard network policies are templated.

customerLevel.networkPolicies.custom.note: MISSING: No mechanism to inject custom NetworkPolicy resources via values. Customers with custom policies need a separate manifest or chart extension. Low priority (only affects customers with deviations from standard).

customerLevel.awsSecretsArn.note: Supported via SecretProviderClass template. Required parameter.

customerLevel.hasSealedSecrets.note: MISSING: No mechanism to deploy sealed secrets resources via chart. 36 customers use sealed secrets alongside the chart. These will need to be deployed separately or via a wrapper chart. Medium priority — sealed secrets are a separate concern from the dotCMS chart.

annotations.linkerd.enabled.note: WRONG DEFAULT: 100% of customers use Linkerd. Default must be true. With false, all deployments will miss the sidecar injection annotation — breaking service mesh connectivity for every new customer.

annotations.prometheus.enabled.note: NOTE: Chart defaults prometheus.enabled=true but 0% of customers have prometheus annotations. This suggests either: (a) the chart feature was never deployed, or (b) prometheus scraping happens at infrastructure level, not pod annotation level. Recommend: verify with platform team; default should likely be false.

envVars.security.DOT_COOKIES_HTTP_ONLY.note: CRITICAL WRONG DEFAULT: Confirmed platform-wide. Chart explicitly sets false. HttpOnly=false allows JavaScript access to session cookies (XSS risk). Fix: change default to true. Validate no customers have legitimate need for false.

features.glowroot.note: PARTIALLY DOCUMENTED: Template supports Glowroot via feature.glowroot.enabled but this path is not documented in values.yaml. 81% of customers use Glowroot. Fix: add feature.glowroot block to values.yaml with enabled: true as default.

features.redisSessions.note: PARTIALLY DOCUMENTED: Template supports Redis sessions but not documented in values.yaml. 92.9% adoption — should be enabled by default. Fix: add feature.redisSessions block to values.yaml with enabled: true as default.

features.telemetry.note: WRONG DEFAULT: 78.6% of customers use telemetry. Should default to true.

features.analytics.note: Supported but minority feature. Opt-in is correct.

service.type.note: Correct. Chart generates NodePort service for ALB.

pdb.enabled.note: CORRECT: pdb.enabled=auto activates PDB when replicas > 1. This is exactly what 71/84 customers need given the TylerTech incident.

structure.multipleEnvironments.note: CORE FEATURE: The environments map with deep-merge via mergeEnvironment correctly handles multi-environment customers. Each environment generates its own StatefulSet, Ingress, Service, PVC, and PV. This is the chart's primary mechanism and it works.

database.local.enabled.note: UNNECESSARY: Chart defaults to local DB container. No customer uses local DB — all use external managed PostgreSQL. Should default to false. Currently creates dead deployment resources by default.

opensearch.local.enabled.note: UNNECESSARY: Chart defaults to local OpenSearch container. All customers use external Elasticsearch. Should default to false.

redis.local.enabled.note: UNNECESSARY: Chart defaults to local Redis container. All customers use external Redis (ElastiCache or similar). Should default to false.
